noraScore.title: Nora Score
noraScore.introduction: Your Nora Score is the daily balance you keep in your child's Emotional Bank Account. It measures your "Connection Capital"—the ratio of P.E.N. deposits to behavioral withdrawals made during your 5-minute Special Play Time.

excellent.summary: Your connection is overflowing! You've made massive deposits today. By maintaining high skill density and avoiding withdrawals, you have signaled to your child that they are safe, seen, and supported.
excellent.science: Your child's Upstairs Brain (logic and empathy) is fully engaged and "in the lead."
excellent.result: Your child's emotional resilience is high today. With a full bank account, they are better equipped to handle frustrations, transitions, and big feelings with ease.

good.summary: You're building a strong foundation. You have a healthy, positive balance in the bank. You are successfully practicing the "Mental Gym" workout and following your child's lead.
good.science: You are successfully balancing Cortisol vs. Oxytocin by focusing on pure connection.
good.result: Your child has a steady emotional buffer. They feel connected to you, which lowers their overall stress levels as they start their day.

payAttention.summary: Relational "leaks" happen. A lower score usually means the "Withdrawal Rate" (Commands, Questions, or Criticism) was high. This is a normal part of busy parenting, but it's a signal that the account needs a top-up.
payAttention.science: A low balance can trigger the Downstairs Brain (survival mode), making children more sensitive to stress.
payAttention.result: When "Connection Capital" is low, your child may have less emotional stamina for challenges, leading to quicker frustrations or "defense" behaviors.

praise.title: Praise (Labeled)
praise.whatItIs: Specific feedback that identifies exactly what behavior you admire. Instead of a vague "Good job," you say, "I love how carefully you are stacking those blocks."
praise.whyImportant: It builds Self-Efficacy (a child's belief in their ability to succeed). By labeling the action, you tell the child exactly which behaviors to repeat.
praise.inDailyLife: Be a "detective" for the Positive Opposite. If your child usually struggles to wait, the moment you see them sitting patiently, use a Labeled Praise: "I'm so proud of how patiently you are waiting for your snack."
praise.contextDescription: These are moments where you named exactly what your child did well — specific statements that tell them precisely which behaviour to repeat.

echo.title: Echo
echo.whatItIs: Repeating or rephrasing what your child says. If they say "Big tower!", you respond with "Yes, a very big tower!"
echo.whyImportant: Known as a Verbal Hug, it provides immediate validation and proves you are listening. It also acts as Language Modeling, showing correct grammar without the "withdrawal" of a correction.
echo.inDailyLife: Use Energy Matching. If your child is excited about a small discovery, match their enthusiasm with an Echo. This proves you are "in sync" with their world.
echo.contextDescription: These are moments where you repeated or rephrased your child's own words, showing you were fully listening and validating what they said.

narrate.title: Narrate
narrate.whatItIs: Describing your child's actions out loud, like a sports commentator. For example: "You're putting the red car inside the garage."
narrate.whyImportant: It creates Joint Attention, which is the most effective way to improve a child's focus and attention span. It signals that their actions are important enough for your full attention.
narrate.inDailyLife: Replace questions with narration. Instead of asking "What are you drawing?", try "You are using the green crayon to make big circles." This provides attention without putting "Performance Pressure" on the child.
narrate.contextDescription: These are moments where you described your child's actions aloud, directing your full attention to what they were doing.

question.title: Questions
question.whatItIs: Even "helpful" questions like "What color is that?" or "How was your day?".
question.whyImportant: Questions are often Hidden Commands. They force the child to stop playing and "answer to you," which breaks their Flow (deep immersion).
question.inDailyLife: Use Active Silence. When your child comes home from school, avoid the "interrogation." Use P.E.N. skills to narrate their actions until they feel secure enough to share information voluntarily.
question.contextDescription: These are moments where you asked something — even well-meaning questions can act as hidden commands that interrupt your child's focus and flow.

command.title: Commands
command.whatItIs: Any instruction where you tell your child what to do (e.g., "Put the block there" or "Give me the toy").
command.whyImportant: Commands take the lead away from the child and can trigger a power struggle.
command.inDailyLife:
The 10:1 Repair Rule:

• Reduce the Volume: Aim to reduce the total number of commands you give in a day.

• The Repair: Think of every command as a $10 withdrawal. To keep the relationship "in the black," aim to use your P.E.N. skills 10 times for every 1 command you give. This repairs the connection and ensures your child is neurologically "receptive" rather than "defensive."
command.contextDescription: These are moments where you gave an instruction or directed your child's behaviour, which can shift the lead away from them.

criticism.title: Criticism
criticism.whatItIs: Any disapproval, including "No," "Stop," "Don't," or correcting a mistake.
criticism.whyImportant: It creates a Negative Attention Trap. If you give attention to a behavior by criticizing it, you may accidentally reinforce that behavior.
criticism.inDailyLife: Use Strategic Ignoring for minor misbehaviors like whining or pouting. Use a "Poker Face" and turn your attention away. The moment the behavior stops, jump back in with a Labeled Praise to reward the positive shift.
criticism.contextDescription: These are moments where you used disapproving language — saying "No," "Stop," "Don't," or correcting a mistake, which can accidentally reinforce the behaviour.

coachChat.psychologistReplyTime: Our child psychologist team will reply you in 24 hours here.

terms.title: Terms and Conditions
terms.section1Body: By requesting to speak with a psychologist, you are asking Nora to connect you with a qualified child psychologist from our team. This service is provided to offer additional professional support beyond the AI coaching available in the app.
terms.section2Body: To enable the psychologist to provide you with informed, personalised support, the following information will be shared with them:
terms.section2Bullet2: - Your child's name, age, and gender
terms.section2Bullet3: - Your child's behavioural assessment results, including primary and secondary concerns identified during onboarding
terms.section2Bullet4: - A summary of your recent emotional massage session activity
terms.section3Body: This information is used solely for the purpose of your consultation. The psychologist will review it before reaching out so that they can provide relevant, context-aware guidance without requiring you to repeat background information.
terms.section4Body: All information shared is kept strictly confidential within our clinical team. It will not be disclosed to third parties, sold, or used for marketing purposes. Our team operates under professional confidentiality obligations consistent with child psychology practice standards.
terms.section5Body: Our psychologist team will contact you via chat in 24 hours. This service is not intended for emergencies. If you or your child are in immediate distress, please contact your local emergency services or a crisis helpline.
terms.section6Body: By ticking the checkbox and submitting this request, you confirm that you have read and understood these terms and give your informed consent for the above information to be shared with our psychologist team.

relationship.headerText: This helps us tailor guidance to your role.
relationship.title: Hi {{name}}, what is your relationship to the child?

childBirthday.title: When is {{name}}'s birthday?
childBirthday.ageSuitabilityNote: * Note on Age Suitability: Nora's method is clinically evidenced to be most effective for children between 2 and 7 years old. However, the foundational skills taught here, such as positive reinforcement and emotional regulation, can be adapted and beneficial for children of any age.

childIssue.headerText: There are no right or wrong answers. Select all that applies.
childIssue.title: How can Nora help?

childSnapshotIntro.description: Hi {{userName}}, Thanks for sharing what brought you here. You are in the right place. We'll ask a few quick questions to better understand {{childName}}'s recent behavior and tailor support to you

childBehaviorProfile.speechBubble: Nora has generated the behavior profile for {{childName}} and personalized plan for you.
childBehaviorProfile.lockText: To unlock the behavior profile insights, you can click here to answer those behavior questions for us to personalize the plan

stable.whatThisMeans: Your child is generally managing emotions, attention, and behavior in an age-appropriate way.
stable.startingPlan:
Spend 5 minutes a day in Emotional Massage — a simple child-led play where you follow your child's lead and stay fully present.

A small moment that strengthens connection and supports positive behavior.
stable.whatToExpect:
You may begin to notice subtle shifts within a few weeks.
Over time, you'll also learn how to support emotions and set gentle boundaries.

mild.whatThisMeans:
Your child may sometimes struggle with listening, managing emotions, or staying focused in daily situations.
Small challenges are common at this age. With the right support, they can improve quickly.
mild.startingPlan: Spend 5 minutes a day in Emotional Massage — a simple child-led play where you follow your child's lead and strengthen your connection — and helps reduce behavior struggles over time.
mild.whatToExpect:
Many families notice changes within 2–3 weeks.
From there, we'll guide you through emotions and boundaries.

medium.whatThisMeans:
Your child may frequently have difficulty with emotions, focus, or cooperation during everyday moments.
Some moments may feel more challenging right now. With consistent support, meaningful progress is very possible.
medium.startingPlan:
Begin with 5 minutes a day of Emotional Massage — following your child's lead in a calm, focused way.

Connection is where change begins — and supports better behavior over time.
medium.whatToExpect:
With consistency, progress often starts within a few weeks.
You'll be guided through emotions, boundaries, and everyday situations.

high.whatThisMeans:
Your child may be having difficulty managing emotions or staying focused in daily situations.
You're not alone — many families go through this. With the right support, positive change can happen.
high.startingPlan:
Begin with 5 minutes a day of Emotional Massage — gently following your child's lead and staying present with them.

This creates a safe foundation for change and supports behavior over time.
high.whatToExpect:
Small changes can begin within a few weeks.
We'll guide you closely through emotions, boundaries, and daily challenges.

wacb.headerText: There are no right or wrong answers — and this is not a diagnosis.
wacb.scaleVeryOften: Very often (more than 6 times a week)
wacb.q1: In the past two weeks, how often has {{name}} taken too long to do things or dragged their feet on purpose?
wacb.q2: In the past two weeks, how often has {{name}} acted up or misbehaved while eating?
wacb.q3: In the past two weeks, how often has {{name}} refused to listen or said "no" to rules?
wacb.q4: In the past two weeks, how often has {{name}} lost their temper or acted physically rough?
wacb.q5: In the past two weeks, how often has {{name}} had a screaming fit or tantrum that was hard to stop?
wacb.q6: In the past two weeks, how often has {{name}} broken things or been too rough with other people's toys?
wacb.q7: In the past two weeks, how often has {{name}} started arguments or teased others on purpose?
wacb.q8: In the past two weeks, how often has {{name}} interrupted conversations or demanded constant attention?
wacb.q9: In the past two weeks, how often has {{name}} struggled to focus or been unable to sit still?
wacb.submissionFailed: Failed to submit survey. Please try again.

intro3.title: Emotional Massage
intro3.description: A simple 5-minute daily play with your child. By following your child's lead and staying fully present, you support their ability to build positive behavior, develop social skills, and improve focus.

parentingIntro.title: Just 5 minutes a day can make a difference.

demo1.subtitle: Parenting is hard
demo1.title: But you don't have to do it alone.
demo1.description:
Your guide to raising confident, happy kids.
Science-backed.
Personalized for your child (ages 1–7)

demo1B.text: Get simple strategies that actually work.

demo2.text: Nora listens to your day-to-day interactions — playtime, boundaries, big feelings.

demo2B.text: Nora helps you understand your parenting style and patterns.

demo3.text: Personalized coaching based on your real moments, grounded in child development science.
demo3.title: Nora Coaching Corner
demo3.card1Body: I notice minor distractions when a little girl cried in the background, and Ziyi got a bit stuck in a "why?" loop. For future situations where an external distraction or minor crisis occurs, you can briefly validate his observation and immediately describe his play like "You are picking up the pink marker.
demo3.card2Body: You did a stellar job keeping questions to a minimum. To further help with his frustration tolerance outside of playtime, try using this same, descriptive tone to label his emotions when he gets upset ("You are feeling frustrated right now") before trying to reason with him or set a limit.

demo4.text: Help your child thrive. Build on their strengths and support their emotional and social growth.
demo4.reportBody:
Ziyi can notice fine details in drawings, such as eyes that others might overlook.

At 43 months, Ziyi demonstrates strong visual discrimination — a skill that will support learning in math, science, and reading.

You can further develop this by asking open-ended questions like "What else do you notice?" when he discovers things.

playSession1.title: Prepare the Right Toys
playSession1.subtitle: To let your child's imagination run wild, pick unstructured toys. These allow them to be the boss of the play.

playSession2.title: Let Your Child Lead
playSession2.subtitle:
For the next 5 minutes, your goal is to be your child's biggest fan, not their teacher.
Describe what they're doing, repeat their words.

playSession3.title: Record Using Nora App
playSession3.subtitle: Set your phone nearby where it can clearly hear your conversation. You don't need to hold it—just focus on the play!
playSession3.cardContent: Let {{name}} lead today's 5-minute play to grow their emotional bank. Easy progress every day, witness {{name}}'s growth.

playSession4.title: Short Play, Big Insights
playSession4.subtitle:
The recording stops automatically after 5 minutes.
Our AI analyzes the interaction and generates a personalized report in just a few minutes

cards.sentenceTipHint:
✓ Great job!
You repeated Ariya's action!
cards.coachingSnippet: I notice minor distractions when a little girl cried in the background, and Ziyi got a bit stuck in a "why?" loop. For future situations where an external distraction or minor crisis occurs, you can briefly validate his observation and immediately describe his play like "You are picking up the pink marker." This smoothly anchors his attention.
cards.focusSnippet: Keep narrating during moments when Ziyi usually struggles — tantrums, frustration, or when he's asking for things. Your narration is already his favorite kind of attention. Make it your superpower.

playSession5.title: Your data stays private
playSession5.subtitle: Your data is always protected and never shared without your permission.

recordingCard.recording:
Recording in progress...
Speak naturally during play time! The recording will stop automatically at {{minutes}} minutes

howToRecord.title: How to record
howToRecord.step1: Place your phone nearby where it can hear you both clearly.
howToRecord.step3: Play naturally with your child for {{minutes}} minutes.
howToRecord.step4: We'll automatically stop at {{minutes}} minutes and give you feedback!

recordingGuide.reminderText: Remember, this is Child-Led Play. Just follow {{childName}}'s lead.
recordingGuide.disciplineReminder: Remember, For every command, aim for about 10 P.E.N. moments to rebuild connection.
recordingGuide.lockMessage: Effective discipline is built on a foundation of a strong bond. Without that connection, instructions can feel like demands; with it, they feel like guidance.
recordingGuide.lockGoal: Reach a Deposit of 80 or more in a single Special Time session to unlock Discipline coaching
recordingGuide.lockBankMessage: This ensures your "relationship bank account" is overfilled before you begin the Discipline phase.

praise.title: Praise
praise.description: Be a Cheerleader. Tell your child exactly what you like about their behavior.

echo.title: Echo
echo.description: Repeat What They Say. Show you're listening by repeating their words.

narrate.title: Narrate
narrate.description: Describe their actions like a sportscaster, without judgment.

followThrough.title: Always Follow Through:
followThrough.description: 5-Second → Offer a Choice → 5-Second → Act on the Choice

readReport.title: Read your report and insights
readReport.description: Check out the analysis of your session.

record.title: Record your play session
record.description: Learning is 2x faster when put into practice. Practice your new skills by recording the session.
record.encouragement: Good job completing today's lesson! Let's record your play session now.

recordAgain.title: Beat your score
recordAgain.description: Repetition builds confidence. Record another play session to practice the feedback you just received.
recordAgain.encouragement: Great work! Want to practice one more time?

firstLessonCard.description: Lessons are short 2 min reads about how important connection is during playtime.

phaseCelebration.title: You've Mastered Special Play Time!
phaseCelebration.message: Incredible work. Scoring 80+ shows you've built a strong, connected foundation with {{childName}}.
phaseCelebration.unlockDescriptionPrefix: You can now get personalized coaching on effective discipline. Simply go to the
phaseCelebration.unlockDescriptionSuffix: , and record a real discipline moment with {{childName}}.
phaseCelebration.suggestionMiddle:
lessons first — they'll give you the framework to make your Discipline sessions most effective.

In the meantime, keep doing Special Time sessions to deposit into {{childName}}'s emotional bank account.
